title: Moroccan Spiced Carrot Salad

ingredients.name: carrots, peeled and sliced into 5 mm coins

subs.name: Roasted beetroot with the same spice mix makes a stunning pink version; parboiled sweet potato works beautifully too

ingredients.name: red chilli powder or mild paprika

subs.name: 1 tsp white vinegar or a generous squeeze of tamarind water for a different but equally tart note
Any neutral cooking oil; a few drops of sesame oil added at the end gives an interesting East-meets-West aroma

ingredients.name: jaggery (for sweetness)
visual_checks: Skip for a purely savoury salad
ingredients.notes: Skip for a purely savoury salad

subs.name: A pinch of sugar or a drizzle of honey (honey makes it non-vegan) for those who prefer a hint of sweetness

steps.text: Bring a medium saucepan of salted water to a boil. Add the sliced carrots and cook for 7 minutes until fork-tender but not mushy — they should hold their shape. Drain and spread on a plate to steam off excess moisture.

While the carrots drain, warm the olive oil in a small frying pan over low heat. Add the minced garlic and fry gently for 30 seconds until fragrant but not browned. Add cumin, coriander powder, cinnamon and chilli powder; stir for 15 seconds just to bloom the spices in the oil.
Pour the warm spiced oil over the drained carrots. Add lemon juice, lemon zest, optional jaggery and salt. Toss gently until every slice is glossy and evenly coated.

Transfer to a serving plate. Scatter coriander leaves and mint over the top. The salad is best served warm or at room temperature — it can also be made ahead and chilled, though the colour of the herbs will deepen overnight.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: carrot → Roasted beetroot with the same spice mix makes a stunning pink version; parboiled sweet potato works beautifully too; olive-oil → Any neutral cooking oil; a few drops of sesame oil added at the end gives an interesting East-meets-West aroma; lemon → 1 tsp white vinegar or a generous squeeze of tamarind water for a different but equally tart note; jaggery → A pinch of sugar or a drizzle of honey (honey makes it non-vegan) for those who prefer a hint of sweetness
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Completely pantry-friendly for an Indian cook — carrots (gajar) are available year-round and all spices are standard staples. Use any cooking oil if olive oil is unavailable. The warm spiced oil technique is similar to a light tadka. Serve as a chatpata side alongside dal and rice, or as a salad in a lunchbox.
